evaluation_criteria.cohesion: Internal edge density and workflow similarity
evaluation_criteria.fk_purity: Foreign key relationships stay within seam
evaluation_criteria.menu_alignment: Groups pages that belong together in navigation

candidates.name: Complete Catalog Management
candidates.description: Full CRUD operations on product catalog
candidates.workflow_cluster: All catalog operations (list, view, create, edit, delete)
candidates.fk_details: All FKs (CatalogTypeId, CatalogBrandId) stay within seam

external_deps.name: Azure Active Directory
external_deps.note: Can be replaced with JWT in modern API

advantages: Single cohesive domain - all catalog operations together
Clean FK boundaries - no cross-seam table references
Clear read/write separation already in code
disadvantages: Mixed permissions (public + authenticated) in same seam
Large seam with 6 entrypoints might be harder to test
Image upload service lacks authentication (security concern)

candidates.name: Public Catalog Viewing
candidates.description: Read-only catalog browsing for public users
candidates.fk_details: All FKs stay within seam (read-only access)
advantages: Simple read-only operations - easy to migrate
No database writes - safer to deploy early
disadvantages: Would need separate admin seam for management
Shared table reads with admin seam (coordination needed)

candidates.name: Catalog Administration

external_deps.name: Azure Active Directory
external_deps.note: Replace with JWT in modern API

advantages: Clear admin-only operations - single permission model
All write operations together - easier to secure
Can be deployed separately from public view
disadvantages: Shared table writes with other admin operations
Image upload service still lacks authentication

rationale: Single cohesive domain - catalog is the entire application
Splitting read/write would create artificial boundaries
All operations share same data model and services
Simpler migration path with fewer seams to coordinate
Can address permission concerns with API design (separate endpoints)

alternative_approach.when_to_use: If security isolation is critical and public/admin traffic patterns differ significantly
alternative_approach.coordination_required: Shared database access, eventual consistency considerations

notes: This is a small application - single seam is most practical
All candidates have clean FK boundaries (no cross-seam table references)
External integrations (Azure services) have abstractions - easy to adapt
Main decision: full seam vs read/write split based on permission model
